Francais

Underground computer art portfolio

darokin

Darokin (Adrien Rebuzzi) is a visual artist and programmer based in Reims, rooted in the early 90s computer underground culture (hack/warez/demoscene). Since 2007, he has developed a practice around pixel art, ASCII art and creative coding, often embodied through sculptures, installations and digital prints. Working exclusively on Linux with free software or custom-built tools, he explores the links between code, memory of early computers and contemporary forms. His works question the place of digital and spiritual dimensions in our lives while preserving a playful and poetic tone.

Instagram @darokin LinkedIn GitHub
